name: KANGLASHA - Guardian of Roots | Oversized Tee
desc: Sacred guardian of Kangla Fort — symbol of protection, authority, and identity. Built for bold presence with structured oversized silhouette.
lore:
Kangla Sha is not just a creature — it is a gatekeeper of power.
Standing at the ancient Kangla Fort, it has witnessed kings rise, kingdoms fall, and history reshape itself.

This design captures that silent dominance — not loud, not chaotic, but unshakable.

The sharp edges, the stance, the symmetry — everything reflects authority without needing to prove it.

This is not fashion. This is presence.
details:
• 240–260 GSM Premium Terry Cotton
• Oversized drop-shoulder fit
• High-density screen print
• Breathable + structured drape
• Built for daily wear + statement styling

⚠️ Check size chart before ordering.
wash:
• Machine wash cold (inside-out)
• Do not bleach
• Do not iron directly on print
• Dry in shade
• Avoid high heat drying

name: KHAMBA - Kao Trial Edition | UT27
desc: Inspired by Khamba Na Kao Phaba — where strength meets calm, and control defeats chaos.
lore:
When the raging Kao was unleashed, it was not just a battle of strength — it was a test of spirit.

Khamba did not fight the bull with fear. He faced it with control.

This design captures that moment — tension, movement, and raw force frozen in time.

The aggressive strokes represent chaos.
The central structure represents calm.

Because true power is not in dominance — it is in control.
details:
• UT27 Unisex Terry Oversized Tee
• Heavyweight premium cotton
• Sharp black knockout graphic
• Streetwear-ready silhouette
• Designed for durability
wash:
• Wash inside-out
• Cold wash only
• No bleach
• Avoid ironing design
• Air dry

name: LAI KHUTSHANGBI - Phunga Wari Edition
desc: Inspired by childhood Phunga Wari — where fear lived in imagination.
lore:
In the quiet nights, stories were warnings.

She existed in corners. In silence.

Not what you see — what you feel.

name: LAI KHUTSHANGBI - She Reaches
desc: She does not knock. She reaches.
lore:
Her reach comes before her presence.

It has already reached.
